qa_pair.question: One read-only operation returns a top-level array and accepts an optional CSV query filter named `tags`. What is its operationId?
A single schema is reused as both the success response body for POST /pets and the array item type for the 200 response of GET /pets. What is that schema name?
Among the schemas reachable from the create-pet workflow, which referenced schema is optional in both parent pet models and defines no required fields of its own?
Considering only operations whose only parameter is an int64 path field named `id`, which operationId has a success response without any body schema?
What property is the only required field in the create request model and is also required in the full resource model returned after creation?
Across all endpoint-specific model graphs in this API, how many distinct schema names are referenced anywhere?
What is the only enum-backed field name that appears in both the request pet schema and the returned pet schema?
Which endpoint exposes a model graph that includes NewPet, its shared nested category schema, the returned resource schema, and the shared error schema? Respond with `METHOD path`.
When comparing the collection-read and single-item-read operations, what optional query parameter exists only on the collection read to cap the number of returned records?

Which HTTP method on `/pets/{id}` references exactly one distinct schema across all of its parameters and responses because it can only fail with the shared error model? Respond with the method in uppercase.
